spec_match.rationale: The page now demonstrates the ChromeStatus parser contract directly: streamed HTML bytes containing <?target data?> are inspected as ProcessingInstruction nodes with target, data, and nodeType.

interactive.rationale: The page has real scenario/delay controls, a live streamed iframe, byte timing evidence, and PI-specific parser probes, but native Chrome verification still needs chrome-devtools-mcp.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept now matches the parent feature framing around parser-preserved processing instructions for streaming markers and metadata.

edge_cases.rationale: The updated conformance suite covers lowercased targets, data round trips, DOMParser, block-listed XML targets, invalid targets, and live streamed parsing.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus, WHATWG HTML PR #12118, and the WICG declarative partial updates explainer.

design_system.rationale: The rebuilt page uses repo CSS variables, responsive controls, and explicit button foreground/background styling.

openQuestions.title: Verify streamed PI parsing in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp to load the streaming-use-case page, reload both scenarios, and confirm the iframe probe reports ProcessingInstruction nodes from the streamed HTML without unrelated capability checks.

summary: Replaced the generic capability probe and static explanation with a server-backed streamed HTML parser demo for ProcessingInstruction nodes.
